title: scan_tx: no bound on number of witnesses loaded into memory
description: scanner.rs CompactBlockScanner holds self.witnesses: HashMap<i64, IncrementalWitness<sapling::Node, 32>>. load_state() loads ALL spendable notes witnesses. Each IncrementalWitness<sapling::Node, 32> has a depth-32 tree; the serialized form is ~1 KB; the in-memory form is larger. For a wallet that has received 10,000 notes, self.witnesses holds ~10 MB in memory. On each block, scan_tx iterates ALL witnesses for EACH Sapling output. A block with 1000 outputs * 10,000 witnesses = 10,000,000 witness.append() calls per block. This is O(outputs_per_block * total_notes_ever) — quadratic in active wallet age. Sapling witnesses accumulate for the lifetime of the wallet unless nie-en5f.2 is fixed. Expected impact: a moderately active wallet with 1000+ notes will see multi-second scan times per block.
